mus.about: MYOD1 and MYOG form a core myogenic regulatory module that cooperatively activates late differentiation and sarcomeric genes in skeletal muscle.
MYOD1 and MEF2C cooperate on enhancer elements to drive skeletal muscle-specific gene expression and fiber-type programs.
MYOD1 and SRF interact functionally to regulate cytoskeletal and contractile genes during myogenic differentiation.
CARM1 acts as a transcriptional coregulator for MYOD1, methylating histone and non-histone substrates to promote myogenic gene activation.
The SWI/SNF subunit SMARCD1 is recruited by MYOD1 to muscle gene regulatory regions, contributing to chromatin remodeling during myogenesis.
TRIM28 functions as a corepressor in myogenic cells, modulating MYOD1- and PAX3-controlled gene programs through chromatin regulation.
MYOD1 and glucocorticoid receptor NR3C1 coordinate myofiber-specific transcriptional responses to glucocorticoids in skeletal muscle.
The RNA helicases p68/p72 and the noncoding RNA SRA form a coregulator complex with MYOD1, enhancing skeletal muscle differentiation.
HDAC4 binds MEF2A and represses MEF2-dependent transcription in skeletal muscle; calcium-dependent signaling promotes HDAC4 export and MEF2 activation.
HDAC5 associates with MEF2C to repress muscle gene expression; exercise and signaling release HDAC5, enabling MEF2-mediated transcription.
The histone acetyltransferase EP300 (p300) coactivates MEF2A on muscle promoters, promoting chromatin acetylation and transcription.
PPARGC1B (PGC-1β) coactivates MEF2C to induce mitochondrial biogenesis and oxidative muscle fiber programs.
NCOA2 (TIF2) functions as a nuclear receptor coactivator interacting with MEF2 pathways to modulate mitochondrial respiration in skeletal muscle.
PPARGC1A (PGC-1alpha) coactivates estrogen-related receptor alpha (ESRRA) on promoters of oxidative metabolism and mitochondrial genes in skeletal muscle.
PPARGC1A acts as a coactivator for PPARA, enhancing transcription of fatty acid oxidation genes in muscle and liver.
PPARGC1A and NCOR1 have opposing co-regulatory roles on oxidative metabolism genes via ESRRA and PPARβ/δ in skeletal muscle.
mus.source: Regulation of skeletal muscle plasticity by PGC-1alpha and NCoR1, 2025 thesis

mus.about: PPARGC1B (PGC-1β) cooperates with ESRRA to control mitochondrial biogenesis and oxidative capacity in skeletal muscle.
PPARGC1A coactivates NRF1, leading to induction of TFAM and nuclear-encoded mitochondrial genes required for oxidative phosphorylation.
SIRT1 deacetylates PPARGC1A, modulating its coactivator activity on oxidative metabolism genes in skeletal muscle.
PPARD (PPARβ/δ) and KLF15 cooperatively regulate PDK4 and other metabolic genes in skeletal muscle in response to nutrient cues.
PPARD and MEF2 family members collaborate to drive oxidative fiber-type gene expression and endurance adaptations.
Actin-associated proteins, including gelsolin, function as androgen receptor coregulators in skeletal muscle, modulating AR-dependent transcription.
SRC-1 (NCOA1) acts as a coactivator for AR on muscle-specific androgen-responsive genes.
Androgen receptor (AR) and mineralocorticoid receptor (NR3C2) participate in overlapping steroid hormone signaling networks that influence muscle gene expression.
mus.source: IUPHAR Guide to Pharmacology: AR and NR3C2 entries

mus.about: Glucocorticoid receptor NR3C1 and MYOD1 co-regulate myofiber-specific transcriptional programs in response to glucocorticoids.
NCOR1 serves as a corepressor for NR3C1 on glucocorticoid-responsive genes, modulating catabolic responses in skeletal muscle.
RIP140 (NRIP1) functions as a transcriptional coregulator that antagonizes ESRRA-mediated activation of oxidative metabolism genes in striated muscle.
RIP140 interacts functionally with PPARGC1A, acting as a negative coregulator to restrain PGC-1alpha-driven metabolic remodeling in skeletal muscle.
PAX3 and MYOD1 participate in gene regulatory networks that specify muscle progenitors and activate myogenic determination genes.
PAX3 and MEF2C are part of master regulator circuits controlling skeletal muscle lineage specification.
